name: Pu'u Ma'eli'eli Trail
tagline: Kailua's lesser-known pillbox hike with the same views and a fraction of the crowds.
parking: Free street parking on Kapa'a Quarry Road near the trailhead
bestTime: Sunrise for golden light over Kailua Bay and the Mokulua Islands
talkStory: These three WWII bunkers were part of the windward coastal defense system built after Pearl Harbor to watch for enemy ships approaching Kailua and Kaneohe Bays. Pu'u Ma'eli'eli means 'the hill of caution' in Hawaiian. The trail follows the same ridge as Lanikai Pillbox from the Kailua side, offering equally stunning Mokulua Island views with a fraction of the crowds. Locals use it as a quick after-work sunset hike — three bunkers and a bonus ridge loop.
